summary: AIR-DROW Localization & Theme Final Edition: every supported visible label re-renders from one bilingual source when the language changes, and each of the six premium skins now has its own paired dark and light palette with explicit icon contrast.
features: Uses a single Kurdish/English dictionary for static labels, dynamic readiness cards, calibration feedback, project UI and onboarding; runtime surfaces repaint immediately when language changes.
Prevents untranslated fallback text from leaking into the opposite language and validates every markup and runtime translation key before build.
Adds six paired premium palettes—violet, pink, sapphire, obsidian, silver and gold—with a separately tuned dark and light mode, readable controls and persistent icon contrast.
Strengthens Android long-press handling across the complete app document, including onboarding and overlays, while keeping real editable fields available.
Retains real camera, hand, calibration, storage and connectivity checks; no placeholder status values are introduced.

privacy.drawings: Your drawing data and preferences stay on your device unless you choose to export, share, or use an optional AI creation action.
privacy.camera: Camera permission is requested only after you choose to open the camera. Camera video is not saved by AIR-DROW.
privacy.handTracking: The deployed app uses the local same-origin hand model and runtime. It does not download a hand model while you draw.
privacy.checks: App readiness and Hand drawing check only read local browser and app state. They do not open the camera themselves or send a report anywhere.

buildPolicy.runtimeNetwork: The deployed browser loads the hand runtime and hand model from the same AIR-DROW site.
buildPolicy.delivery: The complete source package includes the verified hand model. The production build validates the local model before publishing.
